message:
chore(beads): sync issue backlog after review-only cod pass

Flushes the current beads state for franken_networkx, picking up
four new open tasks filed during the 2026-04-22 review-only cod
pass plus two status transitions on existing conformance harness
issues.

New issues (status=open, priority=2):
- franken_networkx-f2e8 [REALITY-CHECK] Native bipartite
  collaboration_weighted_projected_graph
- franken_networkx-njg7f [FUZZ] ArbitraryMultiGraph/
  ArbitraryMultiDiGraph multigraph fuzzing support
- franken_networkx-qm7wl [FUZZ] fuzz_clique.rs clique enumeration
  (max_clique, enumerate_all_cliques) -- code landed in prior commit
- franken_networkx-r1lzx [REALITY-CHECK] Native community
  asyn_lpa_communities
- franken_networkx-y76g [REALITY-CHECK] Native bipartite projection
  (bipartite.projected_graph)

Transitions to in_progress:
- franken_networkx-dyw3 shortest_path Python differential harness
  matrix -- first matrix test landed in 7230abf, work continuing on
  method coverage + callable weights
- franken_networkx-59am flow Python differential harness matrix --
  harness scaffolding underway for the network_simplex / gomory_hu
  family beyond the existing max-flow fixture slice

All entries come straight from `br sync --flush-only`; no manual
hand-editing of the JSONL. Follows the AGENTS.md rule that `br`
never runs git automatically -- we commit the flushed state here by
hand.

 {"id":"franken_networkx-57fr","title":"NX-REPLACE native graph_edit_distance and edit-path bindings","description":"## Scope
The VF2++ common-case bindings are now native, but these Python surfaces still delegate entirely to NetworkX:
- graph_edit_distance
- optimal_edit_paths
- optimize_edit_paths
- optimize_graph_edit_distance

## Goal
Expose or implement native Rust/PyO3 bindings for the no-callback/common-case paths while preserving NetworkX parity for callback-heavy cases.

## Files
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py
- tests/python/test_isomorphism_parity.py","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:22:17.830907891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:15:29.265390204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:15:29.265126505Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"franken_networkx-59am","title":"[CONFORMANCE] flow: build Python differential harness matrix vs NetworkX","description":"Conformance harness target from review-only cod pass on 2026-04-22.

Evidence:
- Python differential coverage is split between `tests/python/test_flow.py`, `tests/python/test_parity_comprehensive.py`, `tests/python/test_rust_wiring_parity.py`, and error-message tests.
- `crates/fnx-conformance/fixtures/generated/flow_*.json` currently focus on max-flow / min-cut / edge-connectivity style fixtures.
- There is no category-level Python-vs-NetworkX conformance harness for the broader flow family, especially `network_simplex`, `min_cost_flow`, `cost_of_flow`, `gomory_hu_tree`, and residual/error-contract surfaces.

Harness scope requested:
- Build a table-driven differential harness against upstream Python NetworkX for `edmonds_karp`, `maximum_flow(_value)`, `minimum_cut(_value)`, `network_simplex`, `min_cost_flow`, `cost_of_flow`, and `gomory_hu_tree`.
- Cover directed capacity graphs, missing-capacity defaults, cutoff behavior, same-source/sink rejection, missing-endpoint errors, infinite-capacity / unbounded-flow cases, and min-cost-flow feasibility contracts.
- Normalize residual and partition outputs where needed, and emit fixtures/goldens plus a category coverage matrix/report under `fnx-conformance`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:16.910419587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:16.910419587Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","flow","harness","review"]}

-{"id":"franken_networkx-dyw3","title":"[CONFORMANCE] shortest_path: build Python differential harness matrix vs NetworkX","description":"Conformance harness target from review-only cod pass on 2026-04-22.

- `crates/fnx-conformance/fixtures/generated/shortest_path_*.json` currently cover only a narrow fixture slice (`shortest_path_query`, `weighted_shortest_path_query`, `bellman_ford_query`) rather than a category matrix.
- There is no category-level Python-vs-NetworkX conformance harness that reports coverage across graph families, methods, callable weights, and error contracts.

Harness scope requested:
- Build a table-driven differential harness against upstream Python NetworkX for `shortest_path`, `shortest_path_length`, `all_shortest_paths`, `single_source_*`, `all_pairs_*`, `johnson`, `goldberg_radzik`, and Dijkstra/Bellman-Ford predecessor-distance APIs.
- Cover `Graph`, `DiGraph`, `MultiGraph`, and `MultiDiGraph` with unweighted, attribute-weighted, and callable-weight cases.
- Lock negative-cycle, no-path, missing-node, and unsupported-method exception parity.
- Emit fixtures/goldens plus a category coverage matrix/report under `fnx-conformance`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:44:38.657859610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:44:38.657859610Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","harness","review","shortest-path"]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-e1f0","title":"parity: forceatlas2_layout should accept backend keyword surface","description":"forceatlas2_layout currently rejects NetworkX's backend-dispatch keyword surface at the public Python API. FrankenNetworkX raises TypeError('forceatlas2_layout() got an unexpected keyword argument "backend"'), while upstream NetworkX accepts backend/backend_kwargs and then raises ImportError when the requested backend is unavailable. Match the public keyword contract for forceatlas2_layout.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:25:21.876931239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:25:21.876931239Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-e21","title":"Extract scale_free_graph parameter sum epsilon to named constant","description":"## What

Replace inline 1e-6 in fnx-generators scale_free_graph alpha+beta+gamma validation
with the named constant PARAM_SUM_EPSILON (defined as 1.0e-6).

## Status: COMPLETED (March 2026)

Line ~1214 in fnx-generators/src/lib.rs:
  Before: if (sum - 1.0).abs() > 1e-6
  After:  if (sum - 1.0).abs() > PARAM_SUM_EPSILON

## Why 1e-6 (not tighter)

This validates user-supplied parameters (alpha + beta + gamma = 1.0). Users pass
f64 values like 0.41 + 0.54 + 0.05 which may not sum to exactly 1.0 due to
representation error. 1e-6 is loose enough to accept reasonable inputs but tight
enough to catch obviously wrong parameters (e.g., forgetting to include gamma).

NetworkX uses the same tolerance: abs(alpha + beta + gamma - 1.0) >= 1e-6.

## Files Changed

- crates/fnx-generators/src/lib.rs (lines 17, ~1214)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.851146350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:02.646456488Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:02.645818911Z","close_reason":"Inline 1e-6 replaced with PARAM_SUM_EPSILON constant.  {"id":"franken_networkx-qj6","title":"Extract min-cut comparison epsilon to named constant","description":"## What

Replace 2 inline 1e-12 values in the minimum_cut/stoer_wagner comparison logic
(fnx-algorithms line ~3197, ~3200) with the existing DISTANCE_COMPARISON_EPSILON constant.

## Status: COMPLETED (March 2026)

Both occurrences replaced:
- Line ~3197: cut.value + DISTANCE_COMPARISON_EPSILON < current_cut.value
  (determines if a new cut is strictly better than the current best)
- Line ~3200: (cut.value - current_cut.value).abs() <= DISTANCE_COMPARISON_EPSILON
  (tie-breaking when cuts have equal value — uses deterministic node ordering)

## Why DISTANCE_COMPARISON_EPSILON

The min-cut algorithm compares floating-point cut values. The epsilon prevents
false "better cut" declarations due to floating-point rounding. Using the same
constant as other distance comparisons ensures consistent behavior across the
shortest-path / min-cut algorithm family.

## Files Changed
